name: Amr ibn Hasan
title: Captive Child
shortDescription: The young child of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) who was present at Karbala and was taken captive along with the Ahl al-Bayt's (AS) caravan to Kufa and Sham.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Hasan (AS) was one of the young sons of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) and was a small child during the event of Ashura.

He was present in Imam Hussain's (AS) tents alongside the other children and women of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS). With his childish eyes, he witnessed the martyrdom of his uncle, his brothers (like Qasim and Abdullah), and other companions. Fear, extreme thirst, and grief made Ashura a terribly hard day for this child.

After the battle ended and the tents were looted, Amr ibn Hasan, like the other survivors, was taken captive by Umar Sa'd's army. He endured the arduous journey on foot and on barebacked camels from Karbala to Kufa, and then to Sham.

In one of the gatherings in Sham, Yazid, seeing this young child, mockingly said to him: 'Are you willing to wrestle with my son (Khalid ibn Yazid)?' Amr, with the bravery inherited from the Prophet's household, replied: 'Give me a knife and give him a knife so we can fight!' This crushing response shamed Yazid.

After returning to Medina, Amr ibn Hasan lived for many years and was a narrator of Karbala's oppression.
quote: (His reply to Yazid's mockery:) Give me a knife and give him a knife so we can fight!
